immediate.solar_wind_efficiency: Solar and wind now provide the lowest-cost electricity (~$0.048/kWh) globally, outperforming fossil fuels in 80% of cases (LCOE data, 2023).
immediate.long_term_savings: Switching to renewables reduces energy costs by an average of 15% over 10 years (renewable-advanced economies), with solar/wind LCOE consistently lower than conventional grids.

structural.jobs: Global renewable investments generated $1.8 trillion and 13.7 million jobs in 2023, creating economic resilience and industry growth.
structural.energy_volatility: Renewables reduce price volatility by ~30% (vs. fossil fuels), mitigating economic instability from fuel price swings.

strategic.energy_independence: Renewables enable $42 billion/year savings in fuel import costs (per country), reducing dependence on volatile global markets.
strategic.geopolitical: Long-term: Lower costs and reduced reliance on imported fossil fuels could lower trade deficits and improve national security.
strategic.climate_resilience: Cyclical economic benefits from reduced healthcare costs (e.g., lower air pollution-related expenses) and infrastructure resilience.

cost_metrics.comparison: Contrasts earlier discussions where solar’s cost reductions were noted but did not fully explore structural benefits like job creation or volatility reduction.

json_output.recommendation: Transitioning to renewables accelerates economic stability by combining immediate cost savings (LCOE) with long-term resilience through job growth, reduced volatility, and energy independence.
